title: Implement email redaction in free text
prompt: redact.py has redact(text) that currently raises NotImplementedError. Implement it so it replaces every email address in the text with the literal string '[redacted]', leaving all other text unchanged. An email is a local part of letters, digits, and the characters . _ % + -, then '@', then a domain of letters, digits, dots, and hyphens, ending in a dot and a TLD of at least two letters. For example redact('contact me at john@example.com please') -> 'contact me at [redacted] please'.
prompt_paraphrase: Implement redact(text) in redact.py (it raises NotImplementedError now) to swap out any email address with the literal '[redacted]' while leaving the rest of the text intact. Treat an email as a local part (letters, digits, and . _ % + -) followed by '@', then a domain of letters/digits/dots/hyphens ending in a dot and a two-or-more-letter TLD. So redact('contact me at john@example.com please') yields 'contact me at [redacted] please'.
